is one of the four administrative districts (*[Regierungsbezirke](/wiki/Regierungsbezirk "Regierungsbezirk")*) of [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), located in the north-east of the state of [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), in the southwestern part of Germany. It is sub-divided into the three regions: [Heilbronn-Franken](/wiki/Heilbronn-Franken "Heilbronn-Franken"), [Ostwürttemberg](/wiki/Ostw%C3%BCrttemberg "Ostwürttemberg") and [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart_Region "Stuttgart Region").

["Tabellengruppe 12411: Fortschreibung des Bevölkerungsstandes zum 31. Dezember 2024"](https://daten.statistik-bw.de/genesisonline/online?operation=statistic&levelindex=0&levelid=1757432774350&code=12411#abreadcrumb) [Update of the population as of 31 December 2024] (in German). [Statistisches Landesamt Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Statistisches_Landesamt_Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Statistisches Landesamt Baden-Württemberg").

["Regional GDP per capita ranged from 30% to 263% of the EU average in 2018"](https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/documents/2995521/10474907/1-05032020-AP-EN.pdf/81807e19-e4c8-2e53-c98a-933f5bf30f58). *Eurostat*.

**Stuttgart** is one of the four administrative districts (*[Regierungsbezirke](/wiki/Regierungsbezirk "Regierungsbezirk")*) of [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), located in the north-east of the state of [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), in the southwestern part of Germany. It is sub-divided into the three regions: [Heilbronn-Franken](/wiki/Heilbronn-Franken "Heilbronn-Franken"), [Ostwürttemberg](/wiki/Ostw%C3%BCrttemberg "Ostwürttemberg") and [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart_Region "Stuttgart Region").

| *Kreise* (districts) | *Kreisfreie Städte* (district-free towns) |
| --- | --- |
| 1. [Böblingen](/wiki/B%C3%B6blingen_(district) "Böblingen (district)") 2. [Esslingen](/wiki/Esslingen_(district) "Esslingen (district)") 3. [Göppingen](/wiki/G%C3%B6ppingen_(district) "Göppingen (district)") 4. [Heidenheim](/wiki/Heidenheim_(district) "Heidenheim (district)") 5. [Heilbronn](/wiki/Heilbronn_(district) "Heilbronn (district)") 6. [Hohenlohe](/wiki/Hohenlohe_(district) "Hohenlohe (district)") 7. [Ludwigsburg](/wiki/Ludwigsburg_(district) "Ludwigsburg (district)") 8. [Main-Tauber](/wiki/Main-Tauber_(district) "Main-Tauber (district)") 9. [Ostalbkreis](/wiki/Ostalbkreis "Ostalbkreis") 10. [Rems-Murr](/wiki/Rems-Murr "Rems-Murr") 11. [Schwäbisch Hall](/wiki/Schw%C3%A4bisch_Hall_(district) "Schwäbisch Hall (district)") | 1. [Heilbronn](/wiki/Heilbronn "Heilbronn") 2. [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart "Stuttgart") |

The districts of Böblingen, Esslingen, Ludwigsburg, Rems-Murr and Göppingen form with the city of Stuttgart the [Verband Region Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart_Region "Stuttgart Region") with a directly elected regional assembly (Regionalversammlung).

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stuttgart_(region)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Economy")]

The Gross domestic product (GDP) of the region was 213.4 billion € in 2018, accounting for 6.4% of German economic output. GDP per capita adjusted for purchasing power was 47,400 € or 157% of the EU27 average in the same year. The GDP per employee was 123% of the EU average. This makes it one of the wealthiest regions in Germany and Europe.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The region is served by [Stuttgart Airport](/wiki/Stuttgart_Airport "Stuttgart Airport"), it is the [sixth busiest](/wiki/List_of_the_busiest_airports_in_Germany "List of the busiest airports in Germany") airport in Germany with 91
million passengers having passed through its doors in 2024.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
